paper_title: Sediment Profile Analysis from Toushe Basin

methods.description: Process for collecting a sediment profile from Toushe Basin and dating it using AMS 14C dating after removing surface soil.

steps.description: A sediment profile (280 cm; 120.90°E, 23.83°N) was obtained from Toushe Basin.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment profile from specified coordinates

steps.description: Remove 34 cm of surface cultivated soil at 2-cm intervals for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: remove surface soil from sediment profile

steps.description: Date the sediment profile using AMS 14C dating.
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS 14C dating on sediment profile

methods.description: Analysis of pollen and charcoal in sediment samples to classify plant types and identify false peaks.

steps.description: Assign arboreal pollen into four groups: evergreen coniferous forest, evergreen broadleaf forest, deciduous broadleaf forest, and shrubs.
steps.searchable_summary: classify arboreal pollen into predefined groups

steps.description: Group herbaceous pollen types as terrestrial taxa or wetland taxa according to their habitat preference.
steps.searchable_summary: group herbaceous pollen by habitat preference

steps.description: Use 37 μm as a separation criterion between cultivated and wild Poaceae.
Use the ratio of microcharcoal and pollen to identify 'false peaks' from the core record.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate microcharcoal to pollen ratio to identify false peaks

methods.description: Measurement of magnetic susceptibility of all sediment samples.

steps.description: Measure the magnetic susceptibility of all the samples.
steps.searchable_summary: measure magnetic susceptibility of sediment samples
